reviewer_stances.reason: The most thorough and specific review, structured by dimension. Its two headline concerns — the causal framing (no causal graph/do-calculus/identification; ANOVA is not causation) and the missing cost analysis — are exactly the concerns adopted by the meta-review. Its originality/scope points (integrative contribution, fixed-answer-only benchmarks, unsupported benchmark-agnostic claim) are corroborated by u71Z.

Specific and substantiated on the two soundness issues that survive to the meta-review: the causal overclaim relative to the absence of a formal SCM, and the sequential-peeking stopping rule lacking anytime-valid bounds (directly matching the meta-review's 'sequential stopping/confidence-interval story needs tighter wording'). Soundness=1 is an outlier, but the substance is high-quality and load-bearing.

Thorough, well-cited critique. Novelty/prior-art on setup sensitivity and ANOVA variance decomposition, the A-MES/GPT-5 circularity risk, benchmark/model diversity, framework lineage on Zhan (2024), and the 500x cost concern are all specific; the cost point was adopted by the meta-review and the novelty/scope points are corroborated by Ppnd.

Partly on-target: the framework-complexity/adoption-barrier and 'minimum viable version' questions align with the cost/feasibility concern that the meta-review flags. But the review is generic and cheerleading in places, and it praises the causal-attribution framing as 'quite original' and 'technically sound,' which runs mildly counter to the meta-review's central concern that the causal language is overclaimed.

score_rationale: The meta-review anchors the profile: an important, well-supported empirical core (justifying subscores of 3 across the board) but with surviving concerns about overclaimed causal language, an under-justified sequential-stopping/CI procedure, narrow scope, and cost — hence a borderline-to-weak-accept posture. Soundness sits at 3 rather than lower because two high-weight reviewers (Ppnd, u71Z) judged the empirical/statistical core sound and treated the causal framing as an overclaim to fix, with Siyj's 1 an outlier; significance is 3, positioned up by the meta-review's explicit endorsement of the message's importance despite two significance-2 scores driven by novelty overlap. Overall recommendation of 4 reflects the pre-rebuttal 'began in a borderline position' framing and the two high-weight 4s (Siyj, u71Z) balanced against Ppnd/Ao1H's 5s. Confidence is 4 because the central criticisms (causal overclaim, cost, prior-art overlap, stopping-rule validity) are strongly mutually corroborating across reviewers and echoed by the meta-review.
